summary: Quality/architecture/DRY/YAGNI slice of CC-0116. The new reconcile_dbcredentials.go is a clean, faithful mirror of the two reference shapes: its ExternalSecret construction matches ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret (reconcile_korc.go:1204) — esov1 API, ClusterSecretStore ref to openBaoClusterStoreName, RefreshInterval 1h, Target.CreationPolicy Owner, SetControllerReference inside the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure (line 106) — and its wait/condition flow matches reconcileAdminCredential (WaitForExternalSecret -> not-ready requeue -> True). The pure builder dbCredentialExternalSecret (lines 52-67) carries no owner reference (verified: no SetControllerReference/OwnerReferences in the builder) and is reused by the test helper readyDBCredES for shape assertions, exactly as the builder doc-comment claims. All five SetCondition call sites set the full 5-field contract including ObservedGeneration: cp.Generation. Naming/constants are disciplined: a dedicated dbCredentialsRequeueAfter (10s) constant with a justifying DECISION comment, and the condition type is the const conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady (controlplane_controller.go:48), never an inline literal, registered in subConditionTypes index 1 and subReconcilerConditionTypes['DBCredentials']. The Keystone projection override (reconcile_keystone.go:123-124) is managed-only (clusterRef != nil guard), mutates only the projected child's value-typed SecretRef (DatabaseSpec.SecretRef is a value field, so the line-113 struct copy isolates it), leaving cp.Spec untouched — corroborated by the dedicated test assertion at reconcile_keystone_test.go:421-422. Docs (controlplane-reconciler.md sub-reconciler table: gate=none, requeue 10s, name {name}-keystone-db-credentials; controlplane-crd.md brownfield must-supply-secretRef contract) are accurate against the code. DRY is good: no duplicated helper logic, remote-key/secret-name builders are single-source. Detected patterns (per-cr-openbao-remotekey-builder, sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration) already exist and are NOT re-emitted. One LOW finding: an admittedly-inert mark_eso_managed call in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh with an unresolved 'Reviewer: please verify' DECISION comment, plus residual 'Reviewer: please verify' annotations in committed test/fixture code.

tests_checklist.item: Sub-reconciler unit tests cover managed-create, not-ready-requeue, ready-true, and brownfield no-op paths (reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go)
Distinctness test asserts both equality of derived key/name AND inequality across CPs (TestDBCredentialRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane)
Keystone override test asserts cp.Spec is NOT mutated (reconcile_keystone_test.go:421-422) and brownfield leaves user secretRef in place
Owner-ref controller flag asserted on the projected ExternalSecret (test lines 123-128)
Helm clusterrole/role tests assert externalsecrets create+update verbs present for CreateOrUpdate

code_quality_checklist.item: reconcile_dbcredentials.go mirrors ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret shape (esov1, ClusterSecretStore, RefreshInterval 1h, CreationPolicy Owner)
SetControllerReference is inside the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure; pure builder has no owner ref
Wait/condition flow mirrors reconcileAdminCredential (WaitForExternalSecret -> requeue -> True)
Condition type referenced via const, not inline literal (no-inline-literals drift guard respected)
Dedicated dbCredentialsRequeueAfter constant rather than reusing korcRequeueAfter, with justifying DECISION comment
Doc-comments are accurate and high quality (brownfield/managed control flow clearly documented)
No leftover unresolved DECISION ambiguity in production Go code
Residual 'Reviewer: please verify' annotation on an inert call in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh

security_checklist.item: G101 false-positive suppressions are scoped and justified (suffix const + condition-type const)

architecture_checklist.item: Sub-reconciler placed in dependency order (after Infrastructure, before Keystone) in the Reconcile chain
Condition registered in BOTH subConditionTypes (index 1) and subReconcilerConditionTypes['DBCredentials']
Managed/brownfield split follows the established clusterRef discriminator convention
Per-CR OpenBao remote key embeds {namespace}/{name} mirroring adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor
Projection override is value-copy isolated; cp.Spec is never mutated
Owner-ref/GC model documented and consistent (ESO owns materialised Secret via CreationPolicy Owner; ES owner-ref'd to CP)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated ExternalSecret-building logic; builder/remote-key/secret-name helpers are single-source
Constants centralised in requeue_intervals.go; no magic-number requeue literals
No speculative/unused fields, flags, or helpers added (YAGNI respected)
Existing patterns NOT re-emitted (per-cr-openbao-remotekey-builder, sub-reconciler-condition-contract already present)
mark_eso_managed on the DB path is admitted-inert (no PushSecret targets it); arguably YAGNI but symmetry-justified

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors from CreateOrUpdate and WaitForExternalSecret set a False condition and return the error for controller-runtime backoff
Not-ready path requeues rather than silently proceeding, gating Keystone until the DB Secret exists

defensive_checklist.item: Value-typed SecretRef copy prevents aliasing the source cp.Spec on override
Brownfield early-exit avoids referencing OpenBao/ClusterSecretStore when the user owns the credential

issues_found.description: The DB-path mark_eso_managed call in the bootstrap seed is documented as currently inert (no PushSecret targets the DB path; the push-keystone-db.hcl policy is deferred to issue #439) and carries an unresolved 'Reviewer: please verify this is intended.' annotation. The operator's DB-credential ExternalSecret PULLS from OpenBao with CreationPolicy Owner and never pushes, so the managed-by=external-secrets metadata marker (which only matters for ESO's PushSecret managed-by adoption guard) has no live consumer. The call is harmless and symmetry-justified, but it is dead/inert work today and the DECISION leaves an explicit open verify request rather than a resolved decision. Recommend either removing the call until a DB PushSecret exists (YAGNI), or converting the comment into a resolved decision (drop 'Reviewer: please verify', state it is intentionally pre-staged for issue #439).
issues_found.fix: Resolve the DECISION: either delete the mark_eso_managed "${db_path}" line (and its comment) until a PushSecret writes the DB path, or reword the comment to a closed decision (remove the 'Reviewer: please verify this is intended.' line and state it is deliberately pre-staged for issue #439's push-keystone-db.hcl).
issues_found.evidence: # DECISION: chose to include mark_eso_managed to follow the 3.2 spec's "mirror | exactly (including any mark_eso_managed call)" instruction and keep symmetry | with the admin seed; it is currently inert because no PushSecret targets the | DB path yet (the push-keystone-db.hcl policy is deferred to issue #439). | Reviewer: please verify this is intended.

issues_found.description: Several committed test and e2e-fixture files retain development-time 'Reviewer: please verify' annotations attached to DECISION comments whose decisions are themselves fully stated and resolved (no open or/vs ambiguity). Examples: integration_test.go:335 and :1086 (harness sync-simulation placement), and the e2e chainsaw fixtures (CR-equivalence and belt-and-braces presence guard). These are cosmetic residue from the authoring pass; the decisions do not leave genuine ambiguity, but the lingering 'please verify' prompts read as unfinished once merged.
issues_found.fix: Strip the trailing 'Reviewer: please verify.' sentences from the committed DECISION comments now that the decisions are settled, leaving the rationale intact.

suggested_improvements: Once issue #439 lands push-keystone-db.hcl and a DB-credential PushSecret, revisit the mark_eso_managed DB-path call so its presence is backed by a live consumer rather than pre-staged symmetry.

Consider a one-line note in reconcile_dbcredentials.go's builder comment that DatabaseSpec.SecretRef being a value (non-pointer) field is what makes the line-113 struct copy + line-124 reassignment non-aliasing — it is the load-bearing reason cp.Spec stays untouched and is currently only documented at the override site.
next_steps: Optional: resolve/trim the residual 'Reviewer: please verify' DECISION annotations in committed code before merge; none block approval.
